title: Kangaroo Level 3-4 - Practice Set 7: Mixed Review
disclaimer: Original practice problems inspired by Math Kangaroo format. Not official AKSF content.

questions.question_text: What is the value of 37 + 45 - 22?
How many sides does an octagon have?
A film starts at 3:15 p.m. and lasts 45 minutes. At what time does it end?
Anna has three coins: 10 cents, 20 cents, and 50 cents. How much money does she have in total?
questions.image_svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 180 60' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><circle cx='30' cy='30' r='20' fill='none' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'/><text x='30' y='35' text-anchor='middle' font-size='12' fill='currentColor'>10</text><circle cx='90' cy='30' r='22' fill='none' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'/><text x='90' y='35' text-anchor='middle' font-size='12' fill='currentColor'>20</text><circle cx='150' cy='30' r='25' fill='none' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'/><text x='150' y='35' text-anchor='middle' font-size='12' fill='currentColor'>50</text></svg>

questions.question_text: Look at the pattern: 2, 5, 8, 11, ... What is the next number?
questions.solution: The pattern adds 3 each time, so 11 + 3 = 14.

questions.question_text: A pencil is 15 cm long. How many millimeters is that?
questions.image_svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 200 40' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><rect x='10' y='15' width='170' height='10' fill='none' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'/><polygon points='180,15 195,20 180,25' fill='none' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'/><text x='100' y='10' text-anchor='middle' font-size='10' fill='currentColor'>15 cm</text></svg>

questions.question_text: Tom is taller than Ben. Ben is taller than Sam. Who is the shortest?
questions.solution: Since Tom > Ben > Sam, Sam is the shortest.

questions.question_text: A pizza is cut into 8 equal slices. Maya eats 2 slices. What fraction of the pizza did she eat?
questions.image_svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 100 100' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><circle cx='50' cy='50' r='40' fill='none' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'/><line x1='50' y1='10' x2='50' y2='90' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'/><line x1='10' y1='50' x2='90' y2='50' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'/><line x1='22' y1='22' x2='78' y2='78' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'/><line x1='78' y1='22' x2='22' y2='78' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'/></svg>
questions.image_description: A circle divided into 8 equal slices.

questions.question_text: What number must be placed in the box to make this true: 6 x box = 54?
A rectangle has a length of 8 cm and a width of 3 cm. What is its perimeter?
questions.image_svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 140 70' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><rect x='20' y='15' width='100' height='40' fill='none' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'/><text x='70' y='10' text-anchor='middle' font-size='10' fill='currentColor'>8 cm</text><text x='130' y='40' text-anchor='middle' font-size='10' fill='currentColor'>3 cm</text></svg>
questions.image_description: A rectangle with length 8 cm and width 3 cm.

questions.question_text: In a box there are 3 red balls, 4 blue balls, and 2 green balls. If Leo takes one without looking, which color is he most likely to pick?
questions.solution: There are more blue balls (4) than any other color, so blue is most likely.

questions.question_text: Shapes repeat in order: triangle, square, circle, triangle, square, circle, ... What is the 14th shape?
questions.image_svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 240 40' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><polygon points='15,30 5,10 25,10' fill='none' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'/><rect x='40' y='10' width='20' height='20' fill='none' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'/><circle cx='85' cy='20' r='10' fill='none' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'/><polygon points='115,30 105,10 125,10' fill='none' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'/><rect x='140' y='10' width='20' height='20' fill='none' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'/><circle cx='185' cy='20' r='10' fill='none' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'/><text x='215' y='25' font-size='14' fill='currentColor'>...</text></svg>
questions.image_description: A repeating pattern of triangle, square, circle.
questions.solution: The pattern repeats every 3 shapes. 14 divided by 3 gives remainder 2, which is a square.

questions.question_text: A notebook costs 4 dollars and a pen costs 2 dollars. Rita buys 3 notebooks and 2 pens. How much does she pay?
How many minutes are there in one and a half hours?
questions.solution: 1 hour = 60 minutes; half an hour = 30 minutes; total = 90 minutes.

questions.question_text: How many small squares are there in a 4-by-4 grid?
questions.image_description: A 4 by 4 grid of unit squares.

questions.question_text: The sum of two numbers is 30. One number is 12. What is the other?
Three friends each have a different pet: a cat, a dog, and a fish. Lea does not have a cat. Ben has the fish. Which pet does Lea have?
questions.solution: Ben has the fish. Lea does not have a cat, so Lea must have the dog.

questions.question_text: A square is divided into 4 equal smaller squares. Each smaller square has a side of 3 cm. What is the area of the big square?
questions.image_svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 100 100' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><g fill='none' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'><rect x='10' y='10' width='80' height='80'/><line x1='50' y1='10' x2='50' y2='90'/><line x1='10' y1='50' x2='90' y2='50'/></g></svg>
questions.image_description: A square split into four equal smaller squares.
questions.solution: The big square has side 6 cm, so area = 6 x 6 = 36 cm^2.

questions.question_text: Kira writes all whole numbers from 1 to 20. How many times does she write the digit 1?
In a sequence each term is the sum of the two before it: 1, 2, 3, 5, 8, ... What is the 7th term?
A jug holds 2 liters of water. Mia pours 350 milliliters into a glass. How much water is left in the jug?
questions.image_svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 100 120' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><g fill='none' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'><path d='M20 20 L20 100 Q20 110 30 110 L70 110 Q80 110 80 100 L80 20 Z'/><line x1='20' y1='40' x2='80' y2='40'/></g></svg>
questions.image_description: A jug of water with a fill line.

questions.question_text: Five children sit in a row. Ana is not at either end. Ben is next to Ana. Ben is at an end. How many positions from the left can Ana be?
questions.solution: Ben sits at position 1 or 5. Ana must be next to Ben and not at an end, so Ana is at position 2 or position 4.

questions.question_text: A cake is cut into 12 equal pieces. One third of the pieces are eaten. How many pieces remain?
questions.image_description: A cake divided into 12 equal slices.

questions.question_text: Luca thinks of a number. He doubles it, then adds 7, and gets 25. What was his number?
